characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: The middle child and only female out of the three Russo siblings. She is sly, outgoing, and usually underachieves when it comes to regular high school. She often gets into trouble because of her constant schemes (usually involving magic). She likes to make fun of her older brother, Justin, with whom she has areally close, complex relationship and she gets along with her younger brother, Max, the two of them occasionally being shown pulling pranks together. It is implied that she is herfather's favorite childand that she shares a tight bond with her mother, although they sometimes have disputes due to Alex's rebellious and disobedient personality. Action Girl: Especially in Season 4. She saves the wizard world from Angels of the Darkness in "Wizards vs. Angels". She defeats Gorog in "Wizards vs. Everything" with the help of her brothers. She even saves the world from an asteroid in "Wizards vs. Asteroid". Aloof Dark-Haired Girl: Tall, dark-haired, and has a cool personality when compared to her brothers. Ambiguously Bi: Fans think she's this based on how she acts around other girls. Producer Peter Murrieta has confirmed this to be true. Also stating Disney nixed an idea to pair Alex with Stevie. Annoying Younger Sibling: Justin tends to feel like this about Alex since she causes trouble that he has to fix.

characters.name: Justin Russo
characters.description: Justin Russo is the oldest son and child of Theresa and Jerry Russo. He is Alex and Max Russo's older brother. He is Giada Russo's husband. He is Roman and Milo Russo's father. He is the erudite and mature older brother who gets straight "A"s. He often tries to stop Alex's crazy schemes from becoming complete disasters, and sometimes unwittingly gets involved in these schemes. Justin is often the subject of sarcastic remarks made by Alex, due to his intelligence, his love of science fiction and other quirks. He is Alex's primary target of jealousy, because he surpasses her in almost everything. Justin is, out of the three, the most responsible. He is very cynical and highly intelligent and is, in many respects,the polar opposite of the immature and mischievous Alex. At times, it is shown that Max sees him as a role model and that his parents are really proud of him. Action Dad: Come "Wizards Beyond Waverly Place", Justin has two sons, Roman and Milo. He also quickly becomes a father figure to Billie. However, he's still just as quick and capable as he was in his teens. Allegedly Dateless: Lampshaded, Justin points out just how many girls he actually has dated and wonders why people act like him getting a date is a great achievement. Always Second Best: Ever since he gave up his victory to Alex in the wizard competition, he's been stuck in Alex's shadow, kind of a reversal of their situation from the original series. "Battle of the Wands" has him being referred to as "Alex Russo's Brother" very frequently, not recognizing his own achievements. Always Someone Better: During their Wizard training and schooling, Justin largely played this role to Alex, due to him actually studying and practising rather than trying to run on just natural talent.

characters.name: Max Russo
characters.description: The youngest of the three Russo siblings, he is the naive younger brother, who at the beginning of the series is an underachiever (when it comes to wizard school), being far behind his siblings in his magic lessons (as seen in "Potion Commotion"). Max is a witty, cheeky boy with a very imaginative and out-going personality. Like Alex, Max is often depicted as a slacker and is been known to use magic mischievously. He is very naive and has a short-attention span. His mother, Theresa sometimes treats him like the baby of the family. He sometimes assists his sister Alex in pulling pranks on their brother Justin. Annoying Younger Sibling: He's the youngest of the three and causes comical frustrations for Alex and Justin at times. Badass Adorable:As Maxine. She not only beats the crap out of Justin, she weaponizes her cuteness on more than one occasion. Cannot Tell a Joke: Max is the reigning king of hilariously-inappropriate comedy timing. Cheerful Child: At the beginning of the series. Later he entersJustin's and Alex's world.

characters.name: Jerry Russo
characters.description: Jerry is the father of Alex, Justin and Max Russo and co-owner of the Waverly Sub Station, with his wife Theresa (whom he accidentally insults on occasion). He is also a former wizard, who chose to give up his powers to marry his wife Theresa, a mortal, due to a rule forbidding wizards to marry mortals. Jerry Russo is the average father. He is stern, protective and is annoyed by Alex and Max constantly, and even by Justin occasionally. Unlike his children, he does not have powers any more; nonetheless, his children inherited their powers from him. Amazingly Embarrassing Parent: Alex seems to think so (and Justin has gone there himself, but not without cause). And Starring: "And David Deluise". Angrish: Usually brought on by Alex. Berserk Button: Standing between him and food.

characters.name: Theresa Russo
characters.description: Theresa is the mother of Alex, Justin and Max Russo and co-owner of the Waverly Sub Station, with her husband Jerry; and is also the slightly more serious one out the couple. Theresa Russo is a typical mother. She's fussy, caring and can be pretty embarrassing. Unlike her children, she is a mortal. She is overprotective of Justin and Max. She tries to relate with her kids, but it doesn't always work. Amazingly Embarrassing Parent Berserk Button: Trying to stop her fromprotecting her kids. Does Not Like Magic:Depending on the Writer, she jumps from absolutely and completelyhatingmagic, to just being cautious, to actually liking it. Fatal Flaw: Her Controlling attitude While Theresa does want what's best for her children, her need to control their lives and force them to do what she wants is what causes them, especially Alex, to rebel against her and use magic to solve problems they are dealing with most of time.

characters.name: Harper Finkle
characters.description: She is the best friend of the show's main character Alex Russo and is often known for her outrageous fashion ensembles that she designs herself. Harper is a typical teenage girl: kind, smart, a bit dorky and a fashion lover. She is very optimistic, though a little insecure at times. Harper has an extremely large and often (extremely)obsessive crushon Alex's brother, Justin. Badass Bookworm: While she rarely ever gets involved with fights, she is seen ready to throw a punch if provoked. She's normally a geek who remains a voice of reason, but it says a lot when she can intimidate a generation of mages. Berserk Button: Being called a bad friend. Beta Couple: With Zeke, once she (more or less) gives up her crush on Justin. Beware the Nice Ones: She's as sweet as they come, but get on her bad side and your be sorry.

characters.name: Roman Russo
characters.description: Roman Russo is the oldest son of Giada and Justin Russo. He is Milo Russo's older brother. Agent Scully: Roman was initially a straight example of this, having never believed in monsters or magic, and is constantly dismissive of the concept. He finds out just how wrong he is. Despite this, it’s still a lot for his logical mind to process that he does remain skeptical on occasion. Ironically, he's better at identifying magical creatures than his brother. Alliterative Name: Both his first and last name start with the letter "R". Only his first and last name initials are alliterative his full name isRoman LaritateRusso. Attention Whore:Downplayed, but Roman certainly has some tendencies where he clearly adores the spotlight and attention,which grew worse after he became a wizard, and tie in with hisControl Freaktendencies. In particular, during the season 2 episode "Cruel Detention",his rivalry with Billie on who is the better wizard reaches its peak when Roman overtakes Billie on the Bibbity-Bobbity-Board -by 1 point, no less- and instantly feels entitled to Justin's praise. He's somewhat put out when he calls Justin, who was busy at the time and thus wasn't paying Roman the greatest amount of attention, in order to gloat, and Justin doesn't instantly congratulate him. He also gets upset when Billie later genuinely congratulates him, as he was expecting her to be upset, and ends up throwing a tantrum that Billie's reaction is not what he wanted, ending it with screaming that she can't take his moment away from him - all because she didn't insult him. Big Brother Instinct:In "Nigh is Now!", Roman instinctively jumps into the path of a blast of energy to protect Milo, despite having no idea what it could do to him.

characters.name: Milo Russo
characters.description: Milo is Justin and Giada's younger son, and Roman's younger brother. Agent Mulder: Milo always believed monsters and wizards were real. He feels vindicated knowing they are. Cloudcuckoolander: Like his uncle before him, Milo has an unusual way of looking at the world. He's overjoyed when Billie temporarily gives him a tail and saddened when his father makes her get rid of it. He likewise admits if he could do it over again, he would go to school on his first day wearing a scuba suit. Genius Ditz: A throwaway line from Roman in "Quest Birthday Ever!" reveals that Milo, despite his eccentricities, gets straight A's. This is a kid who "You Can't Handle the Tooth" reveals can't even spell his own name. Height Angst: He sometimes gets annoyed that his older brother Roman and his nemesis Bella are taller than he is. In the first season finale, he's also slightly upset that Roman's new wand is bigger than his own.

characters.name: Giada Russo
characters.description: Giada is Justin's wife and mother of Roman and Milo. She works as TV News Reporter. Contrasting Sequel Main Character: Like Theresa, she is a self-centered and vain mortal who married into a wizard family. The key difference lies in awareness: Theresa knew about Jerry’s wizard origins before their marriage, whereas Giada remained unaware that her husband, Justin, was a wizard until the show’s second episode. Ironically, Theresa dislikes magic even though she raised her children fully aware of their powers, while Giada—though initially overwhelmed by the revelation—ultimately embraced it, supporting both Billie and Justin in matters of magic. Fiery Redhead: Red hair? Check. Outgoing and fierce? Double check. Former Teen Rebel: Admits in "Something Wizard This Way Comes" that in her youth she was a notorious prankster, to the point of always egging the principal's house each Halloween (and is implied to have potentially started the tradition which students carry on into the present). Lampshaded by Roman, who admits he suspects his mother threw a lot of parties when she was in high school, so probably won't care that much about Billie's. Happily Married: Giada and Justin are very close (especially with her being the best thing to happen to him since being fired fromWizTech), to the point of her regularly flirting with him, even sometimes in front of their own children (to their absolute horror).

characters.name: Winter
characters.description: Roman's best (and only) friend, Winter is an eccentric girl who quickly befriend Billie and ends up learning her secret. Boring, but Practical: As amuggle, Winter’s own gift of intelligence is the least flashy amongst a cast of wizards. However, her large deposit of knowledge, even the most mundane bits, can be incredibly useful in the right situations, whether it be hacking, or making gadgets on the fly for the other Russos. Ditzy Genius: Winter is apparently quite brilliant academically and regularly does projects with Roman, she's likewise got amazing people skills as she shows on occasion. However, her common sense is quite limited, she struggles to read the room, can be quite gullible and has a quite slanted way of looking at the world. Drop-In Character: She regularly turns up at Justin's house out of the blue to hang out, sometimes even when she knows neither Billie nor Roman are around. Justin and Giada often lampshade this, hinting that she should stop, but it always goes over Winter's head. Indy Ploy: If need be, Winter can come up with ideas on the fly, when Justin was in need of accessing the fridge of an exclusive 5-star restaurant, so she comes up with aBavarian Fire Drillto pose as an influencer with plenty of subscribers to get herself, Justin and Billie in, and all she needed was some charm and confidence to pull this off.

characters.name: Zach "Zeke" Beakerman
characters.description: He is a friend of Justin's. He is one grade higher than Justin and is considered "cool", yet nerdy. He attends Tribeca Prep and is in advanced chemistry along with Justin. He is also part of an "alien language" club; he and Justin often "speak alien" to each other. Berserk Button: Mentioning there can be more than 3 meals in a day. Beta Couple: With Harper. Bookworm: Zeke is quite nerdish, enjoys studying and reading. Camp Straight: Zeke has many feminine traits, panics a lot, is whiny and greatly in touch with his feminine side, but he's happily dating Harper.

characters.name: Juliet Van Heusen
characters.description: She is an immortal vampire who falls in love with Justin while he spies for his parents' restaurant. The two fight their parents for their love (similar toRomeo and Juliet, but with a happier ending) and soon, are allowed to date despite the restaurant feud between their different families. When she was born, her parents let her have a soul so she could socialize in the mortal world, which makes her nice and down-to-earth, as opposed to most vampires, who are usually heartless and cunning. Back for the Finale: Gorog brings her back and restores her powers towards the end of the season, as a sort of Ace in the hole to corrupt Justin. Badass Adorable: A beautiful, sweet, kind-hearted vampire who can kick some serious werewolf ass. Cute Bruiser: Juliet's adorable, but she's tough enough to knock a werewolf around. Cute Monster Girl: She can hide her monster traits (wings, fangs etc.) and pass as human.

characters.name: Mason Greyback
characters.description: He is a transfer student from England, perfect in every way except for his artistic vision, and Alex's first true love. He turns out to be a werewolf and breaks Alex's heart when he shouts out thathe loves Juliet. In the end, he repents for breaking Alex's heart. Berserk Button: Trying to take Alex away from him. Big Eater: Due to him being a Werewolf. In one episode, Mason was able to consume all of the meat in the Waverly Substation with no ill effects. Fatal Flaw: Jealousy. Whenever he gets jealous, it causes him to fail to think things through, which further complicates his relationship with Alex.In season 4, during a banquet to celebrate Alex, who was named Wizard of the Year, gets into a fight with Chase, a famous beast tamer who likes Alex, after falsely believing he is trying to steal his girlfriend (though to his credit, Chase really was interested in her), which makes Alex break up with Mason. Hot-Blooded: Mason does have a temper, and it can cause him to seriously lose control of his wolf side.

characters.name: Mr. Laritate
characters.description: He is the principal of Tribeca Prep Sr. High School, which is shown when he calls Alex into the principal's office. He also admires Justin and often compares Alex to him. Born in the Wrong Century: Acknowledged on several occasions that it would better if he lived in the Old West. Likewise, when Alex sends him there he fits in very well and proves to love it. Embarrassing First Name: Herschel. Go-Karting with Bowser: Sometimes Alex winds up alienatingeverybodyin which case Mr. Laritate seems to be her friend of last resort. Kindness Ball: He's usually antagonistic towards Alex due to her laziness and lack of much morals, although a handful of episodes actually showcase that he can be generous and supportive to Alex when the time calls for it. "Alex's Logo" is a perfect example.

characters.name: Dean Moriarty
characters.description: He is Alex's boyfriend in the second season. He makes temporary tattoos in the boy's bathroom and is interested in cars. Dean is shown as a "bad boy", and Justin and Harper do not approve of him at first. All Girls Want Bad Boys: As bad as you're likely to get on theDisney Channel. It's Alex that's really the "bad" one in the relationship, on several levels. The worst thing he does is....temporary tattoos! Bad Butt: Considered in universe quite tough, and does do a drag race, but really only fitting with Disney standards. Love Interest: Alex's. Morality Chain: In one episode Alex's love for Dean is what prevents her from turning evil.

characters.name: Kelbo Russo
characters.description: Jerry's brother. He was given Jerry's powers, when he married Theresa. Annoying Younger Sibling: Jerry's and Megan's doofus younger brother. Attractive Bent-Gender: He has a spell thatturns him Shakira. Beethoven Was an Alien Spy:He's Shakira! He invented the entire person for fun. The Ditz: Quite a dim man, he thought flooding the whole room would be a good idea.

characters.name: Gigi
characters.description: She is (or at least, was) Alex's rival. She has tried to embarrass Alex ever since they were in kindergarten when Gigi spilled juice on Alex's mat during naptime and said Alex had an "accident". Alpha Bitch: Complete with her own girl posse. Chuck Cunningham Syndrome: Was seen once in Season 2 and never again since (probably becauseAlex was becoming a lot worse than her now, so their rivalry had little point any more.) Embarrassing First Name: Turns out her first name is Gertrude. Plastic Bitch: ARunning Gagwith theAlpha BitchGigi was that she'd keep getting plastic surgery. Her extremely loyalGirl Possewould then also get the same surgery afterward, in an effort to look like her.

characters.name: Stevie Nichols
characters.description: A young wizard Alex befriends. She is almost the perfect friend for her.Turns out she is apparently evil and has a diabolical plot to bring an end to the wizard competition, effectively making her theBig Badof season 3. She is killed, but laterresurrectedand has aHeel–Face Turn. And There Was Much Rejoicing: Calling it "rejoicing" might be a bit extreme, but her death was takenwaytoo well by all parties involved. Not even her own brother or the girl who, up until that episode, had been close friends with her so much as shed a tear over it. Although partly Justified as he was unconscious at the time. Anti-Villain:Type III. She was aWell-Intentioned Extremistand she did have a good goal... Arc Villainess:During her arc in Season 3. Butch Lesbian: While not explicitly confirmed to a lesbian, she was confirmed to be Alex's girlfriend and also has a butch style.

characters.name: Rosie
characters.description: A literal Angel, who falls in love with Justin and visa versa. They had a romantic relationship. Becoming the Mask: She originally went to seduce Justin to be able to steal the Moral Compass by pretending to be a good angel, but she ends up falling in love with him for real, which leads her to seek Alex' help to save him and to become a guardian angel again. Broken Pedestal: She was originally the teacher of Tina, a guardian angel in training, who becomes dissappointed when the former joins the dark side. They got better after Rosie becomes a guardian angel again and awards Tina wlth her own angel wings after she helps to retrieve the Moral Compass. The Corrupter: Her presence turns people darker, and her purpose was to corrupt Justin for her Master Gorog. Foreshadowing: In "Dancing with Angels", a good angel manager states that good angels don't lie. However, Rosie initially pretends to be a wizard to get close to Justin and goes along with him, Alex and Harper pretending to be angels to get entry to a angel dance club, which hints at her to not to be a real good angel. This is confirmed when her white wings turn black, revealing her to be a bad angel in disguise.

characters.name: Chase Riprock
characters.description: A famous beast tamer. Badass Normal: Defeated Mason in werewolf form without magic or weapons. Romantic False Lead: To Alex.

characters.name: Master Gorog
characters.description: Theequivalent of Satanof the show (and many other show's). This makes him theBig Bad/Greater-Scope Villain. He is introduced in season four with a plan to turn the world evil by stealing the Moral Compass,which he uses Justin and Rosie for, but was ultimately defeated.He also has a Plan B to take over the Wizard world,and spy's on the Russos incognito throughout the season.As he puts the plan into actionhe is killed by all three Russo children. Big Bad: Of his two arcs in Season 4, "Wizards vs. Angels" and the "Apartment 13B" saga, making him the closest to being the main antagonist of Season 4. The Corrupter: He does this toJustin - though it thankfully doesn't stick.Also Mason and Juliet. Evil Overlord: The leader of the Angels of Darkness. Greater-Scope Villain: Of theDisney Channel Live-Action Universe.

characters.name: Bella Bianchi
characters.description: The neighbor of the Staten Island Russos who takes pleasure in beating Milo at everything. Alliterative Name:BellaBianchi Always Someone Better: She beats Milo at everything, and Milo just wishes he could take her down a peg or two. Sitcom Arch-Nemesis: Bella serves as Milo's personal rival and the bane of his existence. Spoiled Brat: She's quite a brat towards others. Her mother isn't any better, but Justin thinks her mother gets her behavior from her daughter rather than the other way around.

characters.name: Iggy
characters.description: A pixie girl who is Roman's first crush whom he met at Billie's party. Birds of a Feather: Iggy is into many of the unconventional things that make Roman unpopular, like watching documentaries about mimes, and she's just as dorky as he is. Love Interest: Roman's first one, as he's never had any luck with the ladies before her. Roman was so determined to find her in "Battle of The Wands".

characters.name: Archie
characters.description: A former wizard who sets out to find a magical stone of dreams to turn his girlfriend Giselle back to her human form. Anti-Villain: He's a polite and friendly fellow and not really all that evil - he's just devoted to Giselle and serves her because he loves her, and he dislikes the negative consequences this brings. The Dog Bites Back: When he realizes Giselle doesn't have any regard for him as he does for her, he finally gets fed up with her selfish cruelty andredoes herKarmic Transformation- even commenting that he actually likes herbetteras a bird. The Dragon: To Giselle. Foil: To the Russos - his love for Giselle is superficial (since she has nothing good in her personality) and unrequited, with a lot of abuse dish out his way; the Russo siblings might clash, but they actually care for each other and the abuse isComedic Sociopathyat best.

characters.name: Giselle
characters.description: A former wizard, who was turned into a parrot as a punishment for something and relies on her boyfriend Archie to free her with the Stone of Dreams. Fate Worse than Death:To her at least. She turns back to a parrot and once again has to put up with Archie. Flat Character: She gets no context on why she was imprisoned in her parrot form, doesn't get a real personality besides being evil, and gets defeated by Theresa Russo within five minutes. It's All About Me: Shown to care only for herself and willing to manipulate Archie, who is utterly devoted to her, without remorse.It comes back to bite her when he realizes she cares nothing for him, and his morals lead to aHeel–Face Turn. Karmic Transformation:There and back again.

characters.name: Dominic
characters.description: An evil Wiztech member who has a plan to trap all mortal beings in beads and take over the world. He carries out this plan by splitting Alex's positive and negative traits into separate entities. The Antichrist:He isGorog'snephew. Avenging the Villain:For Gorog. Big Bad: OfAlex vs Alex. Half-Human Hybrid:He may be part Angel of Darkness.

characters.name: Other characters
characters.description: Captain Ersatz: Prof. Crumbs (ofDumbledore). Creepy Monotone: Numerous one-shot characters played byAmanda Tepe. Cute Monster Girl: Frankie Stein (no, notthat one). Deliberately Bad Example: T.J. is this to Alex, showing what she would look like if she were actually malicious rather than theAnti-HeroandJerk with a Heart of Goldshe actually is. Goth: Miranda, Justin's first girlfriend.
